title: 'Israel ready to resume war on Iran': Netanyahu reiterates Lebanon not part of truce deal
content: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu declared a fragile two-week ceasefire with Iran, emphasizing readiness to resume military action if objectives aren't met. Despite cooperation with the US-Iran truce, Israel will continue striking Hezbollah. Meanwhile, 254 deaths were reported in Lebanon from Israeli strikes.

title: VP Vance will lead US team for truce talks with Iran in Pakistan: White House
content: Vice President JD Vance will lead a US delegation to Pakistan this weekend for mediated talks with Iran, aiming to secure a permanent ceasefire. The discussions in Islamabad follow a two-week truce, with the White House emphasizing the need for the Strait of Hormuz to remain open. Lebanon is not included in the current ceasefire agreement.

title: Sabarimala PIL was entertained in 2006, but would have been dismissed now: SC
content: The Supreme Court expressed concern over the misuse of Public Interest Litigation for personal agendas, suggesting it would have dismissed a PIL challenging the Sabarimala custom. Justices noted that such challenges, if not brought by devotees, lack cause of action. The court emphasized the need for strict locus standi rules to prevent PIL abuse, especially in religious matters.

title: Cut airport fees for domestic flights by 25% for 3 months: Government
content: Aviation ministry is providing relief to airlines. Major airports will cut landing and parking charges for domestic flights by 25 percent. This reduction is for three months. The move aims to ease financial pressure on airlines. It comes amid rising fuel costs and airspace restrictions. This decision is effective immediately.

title: H-1B visa data reveals how much Meta paid for engineering roles in the US in 2025
content: Meta significantly boosted its engineering and AI workforce in 2025, offering exceptionally high base salaries, with some roles exceeding $400,000, to attract top global talent. Despite a reported halving of H-1B filings in late 2025 due to program changes, the company continued its aggressive AI expansion, ending the year with nearly 79,000 employees.

title: India welcomes Pak-brokered ceasefire, hopes it can lead to peace in Ukraine too
content: India has welcomed a temporary ceasefire in West Asia. The truce, brokered by Pakistan, aims to end a brutal war. India hopes this will lead to lasting peace and ensure global trade flows freely. The government also sees potential for peace efforts in Ukraine. India prioritizes the safety of its diaspora and LPG tanker passage.

title: Anthropic CEO says AI will take your job;but his own company find enough people to work
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is warning that AI could eliminate more than 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs within five years. But inside his own company, the picture looks different. Claude Code creator Boris Cherny says the software engineer job title is already dying — and Anthropic's Head of Growth says they can't hire enough PMs to keep up. The disruption isn't coming. It's already here.

title: China played bigger role than Pak in getting Iran to negotiation table
content: China has confirmed its "own efforts" to broker a ceasefire between the US and Iran, a move echoed by President Trump who credited Beijing with bringing Tehran to the negotiating table. Following a two-week pause in hostilities, diplomatic outreach by China, including extensive talks and a peace initiative with Pakistan, aims for long-term regional stability.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 9
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 9, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Dhurandhar 2 BO day 21: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1050 cr milestone
content: Aditya Dhar’s espionage epic, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge,' continues to script history at the global box office. On day 21, Wednesday, April 8, 2026, the Ranveer Singh-led thriller solidified its legendary status by becoming the first Hindi film ever to surpass the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Having already outpaced giants like 'Jawan' and 'KGF: Chapter 2', the film has now amassed a domestic net of Rs. 1,034.00 crore. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film remains a powerhouse, eyeing a Rs. 1,700 crore worldwide gross target.

title: Musk wants CEO Altman, President Greg Brockman removed from OpenAI non-profit board
content: Elon Musk has amended his lawsuit against OpenAI, requesting any damages be directed to the company's charitable arm and seeking the removal of CEO Sam Altman from the nonprofit board. Musk also wants Altman and Greg Brockman to forfeit any equity or financial benefits to the charity.

title: US desperate for ceasefire? Trump forced Pakistan to broker temporary truce with Iran
content: For weeks, the Trump administration relied on Pakistan to act as an intermediary, urging it to persuade Iran to agree to a pause in hostilities that would allow the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Islamabad’s role as a Muslim-majority neighbour was seen as key to making the proposal acceptable to Tehran. Back-channel diplomacy spearheaded by Pakistan’s army chief Asim Munir culminated late Tuesday with the US, Iran and Israel announcing a two-week ceasefire.

title: Strait of Hormuz: How Trump gave Iran a weapon deadlier than any nuke
content: A recent conflict has shifted control of the vital Strait of Hormuz. Iran may now charge transit fees for ships passing through. This follows a US-Iran ceasefire agreement.  The situation signals a significant change in regional maritime control.  Global oil trade routes are impacted by these developments.

title: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Davey Lopes passes away at 80 after Parkinson’s disease fight
content: Davey Lopes, a former Los Angeles Dodgers star, died at 80 after battling Parkinson’s disease, according to TMZ Sports. He won two World Series titles, earned four All-Star selections, and recorded 557 stolen bases. Lopes later became a coach and manager, working with several MLB teams. His impact on baseball continues to be remembered by fans and players alike.

title: Serena Williams shares rare Easter pics, opens up on motherhood journey
content: Serena Williams shared rare Easter photos with Alexis Ohanian and daughters Olympia and Adira, calling family time everything. She also spoke on Today about motherhood, her daughters’ personalities, and learning to balance attention between them. Her words and photos showed a simple, honest side of life after tennis, focused fully on family and everyday moments.

title: Megan Thee Stallion recalls emotional moment amid Klay Thompson romance
content: The Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson, has proven his love and loyalty towards the popular Grammy winner, Megan Thee Stallion, for close to a year of their high profile romance. While Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson seem happy together, the popular artist had gone through a lot in her life, including witnessing her mother’s brutal death in 2019.

title: Mike Vrabel & Dianna Russini Drama Intensifies Over Scot McCloughan Affair
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini of The New York Times have found themselves in major trouble after photos of them at an upscale hotel sparked outrage and taken the world by storm. The two were spotted relaxing together and sharing a hug which has convinced fans that they are having an alleged affair. Though both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied such rumors, an alleged old affair of hers has resurfaced.

title: 'It doesn't matter who you are': Sooryavanshi steals fresh IPL buzz
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is emerging as a standout performer in IPL 2026, impressing with fearless batting against top bowlers like Jasprit Bumrah. Dale Steyn said the youngster has already “instilled fear” in bowlers. His quick 39 helped Rajasthan Royals beat Mumbai Indians, extending their winning run, with his aggressive approach troubling even the best in the game.

title: From Brahmaputra to backwaters - Assam, Kerala, Puducherry head to vote
content: Voters in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry are heading to the polls today to decide the fate of numerous candidates. Assam witnesses a direct fight between the BJP-led NDA and Congress. Kerala faces a choice between continuity with the ruling LDF and a comeback bid by the UDF. Puducherry presents a multi-cornered contest with new political forces emerging.

title: BJK Cup: Vaishnavi, Sahaja post milestone wins; India beat New Zealand
content: Vaishnavi Adkar and Sahaja Yamalapalli earned their first wins in India colours, helping India take an unbeatable lead against New Zealand in the BJK Cup Asia/Oceania Group 1 tie. Later, Ankita Raina and Rutuja Bhosale ensured a clean 3-0 win.

title: Strait of Hormuz toll? Iran to demand crypto payment from shipping firms - report
content: Under the proposed system, tankers would be required to share cargo details via email before being assigned a transit fee, reportedly set at $1 per barrel. Payments would be made in digital currencies. “Once the email arrives and Iran completes its assessment, vessels are given a few seconds to pay in bitcoin, ensuring they can’t be traced or confiscated due to sanctions,” Hosseini added.

title: Assam assembly elections 2026: Identity, power & the battle of 'Asomiya Jatiyotabad'
content: Assam's upcoming elections are a crucial referendum on Assamese nationalism, identity, and the state's political future.  Regional parties face declining relevance as national parties dominate, with the BJP's 'jati, mati, bheti' plank and 'Brand Himanta' shaping the narrative. The Congress leads a broad opposition alliance, aiming to challenge the incumbent government amidst evolving identity politics.

title: Christians at crossroads: Will UDF hold its traditional base in Kerala?
content: Kerala's upcoming assembly elections see Christian voters, particularly in central Kerala, becoming a crucial battleground. Traditional UDF support is tested by socio-economic concerns, political realignments, and perceptions of communal favoritism, forcing parties to re-evaluate their strategies to secure this influential bloc.

title: BCCI introduces stricter IPL rule, curbs extra players’ movement on field
content: BCCI has introduced new IPL 2026 rules restricting substitute players’ movement. Only the 16 named players can enter the field, while others must stay in the dugout. A maximum of five substitutes can be near the boundary. The move tightens existing rules to reduce on-field crowding and improve discipline, though no official reason has been provided.

title: India's high growth, low inflation story at risk! RBI flags 5 impacts from US-Iran war
content: Even as it is confident of India’s ability to come out of the fresh global uncertainty, RBI has flagged five risks due to the US-Iran war that could negatively impact the economy.

title: Success quote of the day: Greatest success comes from freedom to fail
content: Mark Zuckerberg champions a philosophy where success stems from the freedom to fail, not from perfection. He argues that taking risks and learning from mistakes, even after setbacks like botched acquisitions, is crucial for innovation. This 'freedom to fail' encourages trying new ideas, as demonstrated by figures like J.K. Rowling and Beyoncé, ultimately paving the way for significant achievements.

title: 'Iran begged for this ceasefire': US war secretary Pete Hegseth hails 'victory' over Iran
content: US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth asserted that Iran "begged for ceasefire" following the "decimating" Operation Epic Fury, which rendered their military combat-ineffective. Hegseth credited President Trump for this moment and warned of forceful action to secure Iran's enriched uranium if necessary, signaling a continued hardline US stance.

title: 'Not good law': Centre questions adultery, same-sex relationship verdicts in SC
content: The government has informed the Supreme Court that landmark rulings decriminalizing adultery and same-sex relationships were based on subjective constitutional morality, deeming them "not a good law." Solicitor General Tushar Mehta argued that "constitutional morality" is vague and foreign legal reliance is concerning, questioning its role in judicial review and democratic lawmaking.

title: Gill issues worrying injury update despite IPL return: 'It's something similar'
content: Shubman Gill returned for Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 but raised concerns about his neck injury, admitting, “It’s something similar” to a past issue. Delhi Capitals opted to bowl first, citing pitch conditions and form. While DC continue their winning run, GT are still searching for their first victory, with Gill’s fitness remaining a concern.

title: How Mojtaba Khamenei defused Trump's ultimatum and cleared path for US-Iran truce
content: A ceasefire has been declared between the United States and Iran after 38 days of war. US President Donald Trump agreed to halt military actions for two weeks. This development followed threats from Trump to destroy Iran's civilization if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened. Both nations are claiming victory.

title: From obliterate to ceasefire: How Trump's Iran war strategy shifted hour by hour
content: A 39-day US-Israel war with Iran has entered a two-week ceasefire after escalating rapidly, causing thousands of deaths and widespread displacement. The conflict, initiated by coordinated strikes on Iranian leadership, expanded to disrupt global energy markets and shipping routes. President Trump's objectives and timelines shifted throughout the war, culminating in a fragile pause.

title: PSL team owner contradicts Naqvi on empty stands: Pakistan was...
content: Empty stadiums in PSL 2026 have sparked debate after Islamabad United owner Ali Naqvi linked the decision to Pakistan’s role in global peace efforts. His claim conflicts with PCB’s earlier explanation citing a fuel crisis. Critics say the situation is being overstated, arguing that logistical issues are being framed as a larger diplomatic sacrifice.

title: Carlsen vs Hans documentary: Is 'Untold: Chess Mates' worth watching?
content: Netflix’s "Untold: Chess Mates" revisits the 2022 controversy between Magnus Carlsen and Hans Niemann, sparked by Carlsen’s withdrawal after a shock defeat and the subsequent cheating allegations. The film examines Niemann’s lawsuit, claims of reputational harm, and tensions involving Chess.com’s business ties. Despite polished storytelling, it offers no new evidence, leaving both players standing by their positions.

title: 'No uranium enrichment': Trump ties Iran ceasefire to nuclear curbs & tariffs
content: After a delicate two-week truce, President Trump announced a "strategic shift" in Tehran, highlighting enhanced US-Iran cooperation to dismantle nuclear arsenals. He revealed intentions for both sanctions relief and potential tariffs on countries that supply weaponry to Iran. This interim measure is designed to create room for expansive talks on nuclear limitations and regional stability.

title: Paul Skenes’ girlfriend reveals getting rejected for HBO 'The White Lotus'
content: The former LSU gymnast and social media star Olivia Dunne has recently spoken about her first missed chance in Hollywood. The 23-year-old said she once auditioned for HBO’s popular show The White Lotus; however, she did not get the role. Even after this setback, Dunne stayed focused on acting. She has now secured an important role in the upcoming Baywatch reboot.

title: Yashasvi Jaiswal scripts history as RR star breaks multiple IPL records
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal's explosive 77 powered Rajasthan Royals to a 27-run victory over Mumbai Indians in a rain-shortened IPL 2026 clash. Jaiswal's innings, featuring 10 boundaries and four sixes, propelled RR to the top of the table. His century of sixes in the IPL is a remarkable feat, underscoring his destructive batting prowess and consistent performance for the franchise.

title: Inside the 'strange phenomenon' the NBA is struggling to contain
content: With tanking strategies growing in both audaciousness and frequency of use, can the league get its arms around the often-effective practice?

title: Bryson: Using 3D printer-made iron at Masters
content: Bryson DeChambeau confirmed to ESPN that he'll play the Masters with a 5-iron that he fabricated himself with a 3D printer.

title: Prosecutors seek Tiger's prescription drug records
content: Prosecutors want the times Tiger Woods' prescriptions were filled, the number of pills, the dosage amounts and any instructions that accompanied the pills, such as warnings about driving while taking them.

title: Sources: Kings' late foul was error, not tanking
content: The NBA is looking into what team sources say was a strategy mistake by Kings coach Doug Christie -- and not tanking tactics -- during Tuesday's loss to the Warriors.

title: Rai 'wins,' young Fleetwood steals show at Par 3
content: Eight-year-old Frankie Fleetwood stole the show at Wednesday's family-friendly Masters Par 3 Contest, which saw Aaron Rai win and four holes-in-one.

title: Lopez, Soler given 7-game suspensions for brawl
content: Braves right-hander Reynaldo López and Angels outfielder Jorge Soler have each been suspended seven games for their roles in a benches-clearing brawl during Tuesday night's game at Angel Stadium.

title: CB Moore: Irish on 'revenge tour' after CFP snub
content: Notre Dame cornerback Leonard Moore says the Fighting Irish are on a "revenge tour" after being excluded from the College Football Playoff last season.

title: Dodgers speedster Davey Lopes dies at age 80
content: Davey Lopes, a speedy four-time All-Star who won the World Series with the Dodgers in 1981, died Wednesday at the age of 80 after a battle with Parkinson's disease.

content: Chelsea are reportedly lining up a move to sign FC Cologne forward Said El Mala. Transfer Talk has the latest.

content: Iran War: Lebanon said at least 182 people were killed and hundreds were wounded, making it the deadliest day in the latest Israel-Hezbollah war.

title: JD Vance To Lead 1st Round Of US-Iran Talks In Pakistan: White House
content: US President Donald Trump is "dispatching his negotiating team", led by the Vice President of the United States, to Islamabad for talks this weekend.

title: Choose Truce Or "Continue War Via Israel": Iran Warns US Over Lebanon Attacks
content: White House has dismissed the inclusion of Lebanon in the temporary ceasefire agreement, backing Israel's continued military operation against Hezbollah.

content: Citing foreign articles and judgments quoted by former Chief Justice DY Chandrachud in his verdict decriminalising adultery, the Centre said that "blanket reliance on international judgments is impermissible"

content: A fresh diplomatic row has erupted after Iranian officials accused the United States of violating three clauses of the 10-Point Proposal, the agreed framework for upcoming negotiations.

title: Pakistan And Afghanistan Agreed To Avoid Escalation: China
content: Representatives from China, Afghanistan and Pakistan held a week of informal meetings in Xinjiang, said the Chinese foreign ministry spokeswoman.

title: 'Ketamine Queen' Jailed For 15 Years For Selling Drugs To Matthew Perry
content: Jasveen Sangha, 42, was one of five people charged over the death of the beloved Canadian-American actor.

title: Pune Doctor, 75, Duped Of Rs 12 Crore In Share Market Investment Fraud: Cops
content: A 75-year-old medical practitioner was duped of Rs 12.31 crore in an online share trading scam with fraudsters allegedly coercing him into making multiple investments, police said on Wednesday.

title: Inside 15-Minute Meet Between Trinamool Congress And Poll Body Chief
content: The meeting lasted roughly 15 minutes but has resulted in a significant political controversy with sharply differing accounts from both sides.

title: UP Woman Beats 3-Year-Old Stepdaughter To Death: Police
content: A three-year-old girl died allegedly after being beaten by her stepmother in the Bulandshahr district, police said on Wednesday.

content: US Iran War US Iran Ceasefire Donald Trump Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz In Response To Israeli Strikes On Lebanon

title: BJP's Bengal Candidate Shankar Ghosh Charged For Obstructing Police Duty
content: Sub-Inspector Nirmal Kumar Das lodged a complaint against Shankar Ghosh, the BJP candidate for the Siliguri constituency, and several BJP leaders for allegedly obstructing police duty, issuing threats, and creating public disorder.

title: Why Assam Matters: A State Poll That May Shape India's Future Politics
content: Assam's elections are often viewed as regional, but political history suggests they rarely remain confined to the state. What is tested here, across identity, governance, and voter behaviour, has frequently travelled beyond, shaping broader national

content: Sources told NDTV that the court has validated the statutory process, under which the government blocks access to unlawful digital content under the IT Act and associated rules.

title: India Enters New Nuclear Era As Kalpakkam Reactor Attains Criticality
content: With this achievement, the country formally entered the second stage of the three-stage nuclear power programme envisioned by Homi J Bhabha.

content: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates:  US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-day ceasefire just hours before the deadline he had set for Iran to capitulate, warning that otherwise a "whole civilisation will die tonight."

title: Gujarat Cops Unearth Cannabis Plantation Worth Rs 2.6 Lakh, Owner Arrested
content: In a major crackdown on the narcotics trade, the Panchmahal Special Operation Group (SOG) has unearthed an illegal cannabis (ganja) plantation in Gujarat's Isrodia village.

title: At Least 254 Killed, Over 1,100 Injured In Israeli Attack In Lebanon
content: The wave of attacks began shortly after Donald Trump announced a 15-day ceasefire and negotiations with Iran aimed at establishing a lasting peace.

title: Strait Of Hormuz Reopens After Ceasefire: What We Know So Far
content: The Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical shipping lanes, has begun to see limited movement again after Iran agreed to reopen it under a temporary ceasefire with the United States.

content: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has taken cognisance of the matter and issued a notice to the Health Ministry, FSSAI and Delhi Police.

title: India takes critical step in nuclear dream: How thorium will power energy self-reliance
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. content: An oil rig worker from West Bengal recounts his life in Kuwait as U.S.-Israel-Iran war draws in, with fear, uncertainty and no clear way home

content: Under this model, State electricity distribution companies will pay for installing rooftop solar systems in households that either can’t afford them or don’t have adequate infrastructure to instal rooftop systems

content: Development work at Indira Gandhi Municipal Corporation Stadium is being taken at an initial estimated cost of around ₹170 crore, says Ramprasad Reddy.

content: Kandula Durgesh discusses integrating content, culture, lifestyle and immersive design into tourism development with stakeholders at Hotel Investment Conference South Asia in Mumbai

title: Priyank Kharge directs zilla panchayat CEOs to ensure there is no drinking water shortage in Karnataka villages
content: As Karnataka is facing the peak summer heat, Minister for Rural Development and Panchayat Raj and IT and BT Priyank Kharge on Wednesday directed Chief Executive Officers of all zilla panchayats to take urgent and proactive measures to ensure that no village faces a shortage of drinking water during the summer months

title: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah accuses Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has accused the Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’ in its approach

title: All set for polling for Davangere South, Bagalkot byelections on April 9
content: A total of 34 candidates are in fray for the byelection including nine in Bagalkot and 25 in Davangere South constituency

title: EPS has no achievements to speak of during poll campaign, makes false accusations, says Stalin
content: The DMK president blamed the AIADMK leader for trying to scuttle Ungaludan Stalin initiative when it was introduced, by filing a petition in the court against it

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Two women athletes injured, one critical, after car carrying UDF workers hit them
content: The accident occurred on the Asramam Link Road shortly after the public campaign concluded at 6 p.m on Tuesday.

title: India to engage new government of Bangladesh constructively, to deepen ties, says Jaishankar
content: Relations will not be “held hostage” to one issue, say Bangladeshi sources privy to the discussion, referring to the issue of Sheikh Hasina’s presence in India

title: No devotee of Ayyappa would have moved SC challenging exclusion of women of menstruating age: Justice Nagarathna
content: The judge says the question whether a stranger to a denomination could question a practice in a public interest petition ought to be taken up as the first issue to be decided by the Sabarimala Bench

title: Special session on delimitation: T.N. CM Stalin says ‘DMK will not stand by and watch’
content: Stalin asked why there is a hurry to convene a special session of Parliament right in the middle of elections to five States

title: Cabinet clears draft amendment Bill to implement women’s quota
content: It proposes delimitation based on 2011 Census and expansion of Lok Sabha strength from 543 to 816 seats

title: PM Modi wants collective action on women’s reservation, wants all MPs to back amendment Bill
content: The Prime Minister says the 2029 Lok Sabha election and Assembly polls in the future must be held with provisions for women’s reservation

title: Pawan Khera accuses Assam CM Himanta of using the police to silence questions
content: In a recorded video message from an undisclosed location, the Congress leader says his party has only sought answers and would continue to do so

title: Army Chief visits HAL, takes sortie in Prachand
content: The Army Chief assessed indigenous platforms, including the Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH) Dhruv and the Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) Prachand, along with the upgrade, maintenance and production ecosystems supporting Army Aviation.

title: Make-or-break questions the USWNT must answer before next year's World Cup
content: the 2027 women's world cup is just over a year away . the team is about to play the same team three times in a week . defender Tierna Davidson is back with the team for the first time in 17 months after her maternity leave .

title: Bombay HC quashes ECI notification for Goa’s Ponda bye-election, day before polling
content: Congress leader and Central Working Committee member Girish Chodankar said the party will file a special leave petition in the Supreme Court against the High Court decision.

title: Surprised that NCERT singled out three experts on book row: Alok Prasanna Kumar
content: Mr. Kumar, who drafted the controversial chapter ‘Role of Judiciary in Society’ and a sub-section on ‘Corruption in Judiciary’ in the now-withdrawn Class 8 textbook, said there has been ‘no selective targeting of judiciary’

title: Karnataka’s groundwater extraction reduced from 68.44% in 2024 to 66.49% in 2025
content: Releasing the 2025 Groundwater Assessment Report here on Wednesday, Karnataka Minister Boseraju says gains had been made owing to good rainfall, policy interventions, and large-scale water conservation initiatives implemented over the past two years

title: Centre raises fertilizer subsidy for kharif season
content: Centre increases fertilizer subsidy by 12% for kharif season to ensure affordable prices amid global price volatility concerns

title: Delhi High Court terms tweets by journalist Rana Ayyub ‘derogatory and communal’
content: Petitioner Amita Sachdeva alleges that the journalist’s tweets, posted in 2013-2017, insulted Hindu deities and ‘revered historical figures’

title: Jacobite Church demands action over tension at St. Mary’s Church in Kattachira
content: Church alleges that the police tried to stop parish members from entering the St. Mary’s Church in violation of the provisions under the Kerala Christian Cemeteries (Right to Burial of Corpse) Act

title: India withdraws bid to host COP 33 in 2028: Report
content: No official statement yet; Prime Minister Narendra Modi had announced India’s interest in hosting the summit at COP 28 in Dubai in 2023

title: Kannada activist Vatal Nagaraj arrested amidst high drama in a case over a protest dating back to 2021
content: It is a travesty that such a petty case is being dragged on for so long by the government and even after five years, the case is still going on and I was arrested, says Vatal Nagaraj

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now
content: the 2026-27 college basketball season is expected to take place in 2026 . we're already rolling right into the "offseason," which mostly involves rampant roster changes for the next six weeks . in the past, we've had pretty hard-and-fast house rules regarding the Way-Too-Early Top 25 .

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal officially opened Monday . by day's end more than 1,000 players had entered the portal . new regulations this year prevented any official activity until after the season concluded Sunday . the transfer portal window is also shorter this year, closing on April 20 .

title: WNBA mock draft: UConn's Fudd drops to No. 3 pick
content: Olivia Miles drops 28 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists as TCU puts away Virginia 79-69 . the college players available in Monday's draft had one last audition in the tournament . in our latest WNBA mock draft, for example, Miles projected as a lottery pick last season when she was eligible age-wise .

title: Frozen Four: How Denver, Michigan, Wisconsin and North Dakota can win it all
content: the 2026 men's Frozen Four is set, with Michigan and Denver joining North Dakota and Wisconsin in the field bound for Las Vegas to play for the national championship . the Pioneers avenged last season's loss to Western Michigan with a 6-2 win over the defending national champs to win the Loveland Regional .

title: 'I'm going to put it in my house in a safe': How the Masters lawn gnome became so popular that it might be discontinued
content: Ryan Carey is considered the world's most accomplished golf memorabilia expert . the gnome has been a kitschy decorative item for centuries . it's the only item available on site that is limited to one purchase per patron .

title: Rankings: Profiling more than 60 wide receivers
content: Mike Clay is revealing his fantasy football rookie rankings at each key offensive position . the rankings do not include rookies because we are unsure of their landing spots . all references to player ages are as of the season opener on sept. 9, 2026 .

title: 'Don't move on, just move forward': How hockey helped Michigan's Michael Hage overcome tragedy
content: the path to a national title runs through the site of the Frozen Four . it was in Las Vegas where one of the most significant moments of Hage's hockey career occurred . the 19-year-old center doesn't remember everything about his draft night at the Sphere in June 2024 .

title: Falcons pivot after RT McGary retires, add Taylor
content: RT McGary is retiring at age 31 . he was scheduled to cover the blind side of left-handed quarterback Michael Penix Jr . the 31-year-old missed the entire 2025 season with a knee injury .

title: 'We expect unimpeded freedom of navigation': India welcomes US-Iran ceasefire
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: NBA Power Rankings: Where all 30 teams land
content: the end of the 2025-26 regular season is finally here . the campaign officially coming to a close this Sunday with all 30 teams playing . every new season comes with high expectations, with some teams meeting or exceeding those goals and others falling far from the original game plan .
